features.name: Mechanical Handle Locking
features.rationale: Commodity FP baseline. Primary defense against MC resident elopement. Mechanical operation eliminates power-dependency failure mode. Safety (9), Reliability (8), Confidence (8).

features.name: Audible Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Commodity FP baseline. Immediate audible alert is primary near-field notification for on-unit staff. Power-independent piezoelectric horn directly addresses N07 (power-fail alarm reliability). Safety (9), Pain (7), Confidence (7).

features.name: Single-Motion Emergency Release
features.rationale: EN 179/EN 1125 and NFPA 101 mandate single-motion escape. In CCRC with mobility-limited residents, staff-assisted evacuation depends entirely on this. Safety (10), Skill (8), Confidence (8).

features.name: Key Override Bypass
features.rationale: One_dimensional: more keys distributed to staff = fewer false alarms during authorized care rounds in secured MC corridors. Stress (8): night-shift CNAs highly stressed by alarm events; silent key access directly reduces anxiety.

features.name: Battery-Powered Operation
features.rationale: Attractive — genuine differentiator vs. wired alternatives dominant in CCRC. Eliminates electrician work in occupied care wings. Cost (8), Skill (8), Pain (7), Delight (7). Battery depletion risk moderates Reliability and Confidence.

features.name: Pre-Alarm Warning
features.rationale: Below-average fit. Valuable in IL/AL/SNF zones (reduces alarm fatigue). Counterproductive in Memory Care: initial light contact delays full 95 dB activation during elopement attempt. Safety (4) reflects net-neutral to negative effect.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of IoT module battery depletion going undetected on an emergency exit door in a low-traffic CCRC wing during overnight staffing periods
Minimize the likelihood of wireless alarm event transmission failure from emergency exit doors in reinforced concrete corridors exceeding the 30 m integrated transmitter range
Minimize the time to integrate EasyWave-protocol door guardian event streams into an existing BACnet or KNX building management platform
Minimize the likelihood of unauthorized guard removal or mechanism defeat by a cognitively impaired resident who manipulates the exposed surface-mounted housing
Minimize the time to discover that a wireless receiver unit serving multiple door guardians on a single care level has lost connectivity
